question.text: On premature exit from NPS Tier 1 before age 60, what percentage of the accumulated corpus must be used to purchase an annuity?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** If a subscriber exits NPS Tier 1 before age 60, 80% of the accumulated corpus must be used to purchase an annuity from an empanelled insurance company. Only the remaining 20% can be withdrawn as a lump sum. This is more restrictive than normal retirement exit where only 40% must go to annuity. **The Trap:** 40% is the annuity requirement at normal retirement (age 60), and candidates frequently confuse the two scenarios. The question specifically asks about premature exit, which has a stricter 80% annuity mandate. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A tests NPS withdrawal rules; NISM Series V-A covers pension fund concepts at a basic level. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 80% annuity requirement on premature exit is a powerful deterrent -- use it to counsel clients against withdrawing early from NPS, as annuity income is fully taxable and the client loses flexibility over 80% of their corpus.
